rulings.Case Title: BETTY WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 75, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 30, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant B.E. Development Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden by demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objections to Exhibits D and E are sustained and its objections to Exhibits J and K are overruled. Even if the Court were to consider plaintiff's Exhibits D and E, summary adjudication of the punitive damages claim would be granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KEAYS ET AL VS. 50 BEALE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 30, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Foster Wheeler Energy Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs named defendant individually and as successor-in-interest to Barsotti's, Inc. As to plaintiffs' successor liability claims against defendant, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant is liable for the liabilities of Barsotti's, Inc. As to plaintiffs' claims against defendant individually, plaintiffs concede that their sole claim against defendant is based on alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to Barsotti's, Inc.; plaintiffs make clear they do not allege that Mrs. Keays was exposed to a Foster Wheeler product. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES EVANS JR. ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 30, 2016 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Victaulic Company's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar pursuant to the notice of the death of plaintiff Charles Evans, Jr. as well as the dismissal of defendant filed March 22, 2016. = (503/GLW)
